data.narrative:
Portal Space Systems is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $62.85M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four private peers at same or adjacent stages—Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B)—alongside five public Space & Aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods apply here. The stage-progression approach, anchoring to $63M in total raised and applying a typical 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $126M. The peer-median method yields $400M, drawing from the four private comparables with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring does not apply due to absence of target revenue data. These two methods produce a median implied valuation of $263M, with a low-end support at $107M (stage-progression floor) and a high-end ceiling at $460M (peer-median upper bound).

Key uncertainties include Portal Space Systems' revenue trajectory relative to peers, capital intensity of operations, and near-term funding cycle timing. The $107M–$460M range reflects meaningful dispersion, driven chiefly by the gap between early-stage capital-based valuation and comparable peer medians. A Series A valuation in the $200M–$350M band would position the company within peer norms while reflecting early-stage risk.
